questions.q: By what English nickname is the Jahaz Mahal popularly known?
questions.explanation: Jahaz Mahal translates to 'Ship Palace' because its long, narrow form appears to float like a ship between two lakes.

questions.q: In which historic city is the Jahaz Mahal located?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal is one of the most famous monuments of the medieval fortress city of Mandu.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state does the Jahaz Mahal stand?
questions.explanation: Mandu, and therefore the Jahaz Mahal, is located in the Malwa region of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Why is the Jahaz Mahal said to resemble a ship?
options: It is shaped like a sail
It is built on a boat-shaped platform of stone
It is a long, narrow building set between two lakes
It has a prow carved at one end
questions.explanation: The long and narrow palace sits on a strip of land between two lakes, making it look like a ship floating on water.

questions.q: Between how many lakes is the Jahaz Mahal situated?
questions.explanation: The palace stands on a narrow strip of land between two lakes, the Munj Talao and the Kapur Talao.

questions.q: During which broad period of Indian history was the Jahaz Mahal built?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal was built during the medieval period under the Malwa Sultanate.

questions.q: The Jahaz Mahal is most associated with the court of which sultan?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal is traditionally linked to Sultan Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji of Malwa, for whose court it served.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the Jahaz Mahal?
questions.explanation: Like most of Mandu's monuments, the Jahaz Mahal is built of local stone masonry.

questions.q: What kind of structure is the Jahaz Mahal?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal is a palace, built as part of the royal complex at Mandu.

questions.q: Mandu, where the Jahaz Mahal stands, lies in which historic region of central India?
questions.explanation: Mandu is the famous hilltop fortress city of the Malwa region in central India.

questions.q: Between which two artificial lakes does the Jahaz Mahal lie?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal sits on the narrow neck of land between the Munj Talao and the Kapur Talao at Mandu.

questions.q: Roughly how long is the Jahaz Mahal, contributing to its elongated 'ship' appearance?
questions.explanation: The palace is around 120 metres long but only about 15 metres wide, giving it its distinctive ship-like proportions.

questions.q: Approximately how many storeys does the Jahaz Mahal have?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal is a double-storeyed palace, with open pavilions and terraces above.

questions.q: To which ruling dynasty of Malwa did Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji, builder of the Jahaz Mahal's court, belong?
questions.explanation: Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji belonged to the Khalji dynasty that ruled the independent Sultanate of Malwa from Mandu.

questions.q: The Jahaz Mahal is traditionally said to have been built to house what?
options: A large royal harem and women of the court
questions.explanation: The palace is traditionally described as a pleasure retreat housing the large harem of Sultan Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji's court.

questions.q: In which century is the Jahaz Mahal generally dated to its construction?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal was built in the latter half of the 15th century during the reign of Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji.

questions.q: Which feature crowns the rooftop terrace of the Jahaz Mahal, giving it a graceful skyline?
questions.explanation: Airy domed pavilions and kiosks crown the terrace, where the court could enjoy breezes and views over the lakes.

questions.q: Water management was central to the Jahaz Mahal's design. Which of these is a genuine feature of the palace?
options: An underground river tunnel to the Narmada
Cisterns, channels and ornamental pools fed with water
questions.explanation: The palace featured cisterns, water channels and pools, including a notable spiral channel, reflecting Mandu's sophisticated water engineering.

questions.q: The architecture of the Jahaz Mahal is an example of which regional style?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal exemplifies the austere yet elegant provincial Indo-Islamic architecture of the Malwa Sultanate at Mandu.

questions.q: Which body is responsible for the protection and conservation of the Jahaz Mahal today?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal, as part of the Mandu monuments, is a protected site under the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: The Jahaz Mahal is part of the royal enclave at Mandu, which also includes which neighbouring palace?
questions.explanation: The Jahaz Mahal stands close to the Hindola Mahal (the 'Swinging Palace') within Mandu's royal complex.

questions.q: What gives the Jahaz Mahal its illusion of floating, especially during the monsoon?
options: The lakes on either side filling with water
questions.explanation: When the flanking lakes fill during the rains, the long palace appears to float like a ship upon the water.

questions.q: What was the contemporary name of Mandu during the Sultanate period?
questions.explanation: Mandu was known as Shadiabad, meaning the 'City of Joy', a name given during the Malwa Sultanate.

questions.q: Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji, linked to the Jahaz Mahal, was the son and successor of which Malwa sultan?
questions.explanation: Ghiyas-ud-din Khalji succeeded his father Mahmud Khalji I (Mahmud Shah I) as sultan of Malwa.

questions.q: Mandu's later romance, often associated with its monuments in popular memory, involves which sultan and his beloved?
questions.explanation: Mandu is famed for the legend of Sultan Baz Bahadur and the singer-queen Rani Roopmati, central to the city's romantic lore.

questions.q: The Munj Talao lake beside the Jahaz Mahal is traditionally named after which Paramara king of Malwa?
questions.explanation: The Munj Talao is associated by tradition with Munja, a celebrated Paramara king of Malwa from before the Sultanate era.

questions.q: A distinctive water feature near the Jahaz Mahal is a spiral channel known by what descriptive name?
options: The serpentine or spiral water channel
questions.explanation: The palace complex includes a winding spiral (serpentine) water channel that carried water through the terraces and pools.

questions.q: Mandu, the home of the Jahaz Mahal, is situated on a plateau that is a spur of which mountain range?
questions.explanation: Mandu sits atop a plateau of the Vindhya Range overlooking the Nimar plains and the Narmada valley.

questions.q: The independent Sultanate of Malwa, which built the Jahaz Mahal, broke away from which larger northern sultanate?
questions.explanation: Malwa became an independent sultanate in the early 15th century as the authority of the Delhi Sultanate weakened after Timur's invasion.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor, who visited Mandu, recorded admiration for the monuments and water palaces of the city?
questions.explanation: Emperor Jahangir stayed at Mandu and described its palaces and lakes in his memoirs, helping preserve the city's fame.
